name: Distributron

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + exactly 1 selected shape layer containing at least one path
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem; reads comp.selectedLayers and expects exactly 1 shape layer; reads all other layers in the comp as distributable targets; reads the shape layer's Anchor Point value to position the CTRL null; reads the shape layer's index to determine distribution range (minIndex = shapeLayer.index + 1, maxIndex = layers.length).

controls.name: Randomize Rotation
controls.role: adds random rotation variation (±value degrees) to each distributed layer independently

controls.name: Rotation Offset
controls.role: sets a global rotation offset (degrees) added to every distributed layer's path-tangent angle

controls.name: Start Distribution Offset
controls.role: shifts the start of the distribution range along the path (pixels from path start)

controls.name: End Distribution Offset
controls.role: shrinks the end of the distribution range along the path (pixels from path end)

controls.name: Distribution setup
controls.role: group header that contains the First/Last Layer Index and Distribution Path Layer controls

controls.name: First Layer Index
controls.role: sets the comp layer index of the first layer to include in the distribution

controls.name: Last Layer Index
controls.role: sets the comp layer index of the last layer to include in the distribution

controls.name: Distribution Path Layer
controls.role: references the shape layer whose mask path is used as the distribution path

functions: guard: alerts and exits when the number of selected layers is not exactly 1
move the selected shape layer to index 1 if it is not already there
add a Mask to the shape layer and set its Mask Path expression to follow the first shape group path
create a CTRL null layer and parent it to the shape layer
position the CTRL null at the shape layer's anchor point
apply the 'Distribute Along the Path' pseudo-effect FFX binary to the CTRL null via a temp file
set the Distribution Path Layer effect control to the shape layer's index
set First Layer Index and Last Layer Index controls to the distributable layer range
for each distributable layer: parent it to the CTRL null
for each distributable layer: apply the path-following expression to its Position property
for each distributable layer: apply the path-following expression to its Rotation property
wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'Distributron'

scriptSim.scenario: success — distribute 3 layers along shape path
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Path Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 5s); 1 selected shape layer 'Path Shape' at index 1 with a shape group path; distributable layers 'Item A' (index 2), 'Item B' (index 3), 'Item C' (index 4)

operations.target: CTRL/Effects/Distribute Along the Path/Distribution Path Layer
CTRL/Effects/Distribute Along the Path/First Layer Index
CTRL/Effects/Distribute Along the Path/Last Layer Index

sim.note: Sweeps Start Distribution Offset from 0 to 10 (pixels along path); output is the 2D position on the path for 'Item A' (index 2, first of 3 distributed layers). As startOffset increases, the distributed position slides forward along the path. The effective path length shrinks by the same amount.

Sweeps Rotation Offset from -180 to +180 degrees; output is the rotation of 'Item A' in degrees, which is the path tangent angle at its position plus 90 degrees plus the offset. Curve is linear (y = pathTangentAngle + 90 + x) for a fixed path position.

notes: Script is HEADLESS — it runs inline with no ScriptUI window; all controls are pseudo-effect sliders on the generated CTRL null layer, manipulated from the AE Effects panel.
The same expression string is applied to both Position and Rotation of every distributed layer; the expression branches on thisProperty.name to return pos or rotation respectively.
The pseudo-effect FFX binary is written to Folder.temp at runtime and applied via layer.applyPreset(); this requires AE's 'Allow Scripts to Write Files and Access Network' preference to be enabled.
randomRotation uses Math.random() inside the expression, which re-evaluates every frame; this causes flickering unless Randomize Rotation is set to 0.

The mask expression 'thisLayer.content(1).content(1).path' hard-codes the first shape group's first content item; if the shape layer has additional groups or reordered contents, the mask may reference the wrong path.
The CTRL null is parented to the shape layer so it moves with the shape; all distributed layers are parented to CTRL so the whole rig moves together.
Shape layer is moved to comp index 1 (moveToBeginning) if it is not already there, which may reorder other layers.
First Layer Index and Last Layer Index are set automatically at script-run time; they can be animated post-run to change which layer range is distributed.
